task_intent: Scientific-answer test: compare binding of two apolar ligands to the engineered T4 lysozyme L99A cavity and report which binds more tightly. Compare the benzene complex (PDB 4W52, also 181L) with the n-butylbenzene complex (PDB 4W57) using short comparative MD, and report effect.direction as stronger_binding, weaker_binding, or similar for n-butylbenzene RELATIVE TO benzene. Adding hydrophobic surface tightens binding in the apolar cavity (Morton, Baase & Matthews 1995), so n-butylbenzene binds more strongly than benzene. Populate metrics.md_analysis with interaction evidence (ligand-cavity contact counts, buried apolar surface, ligand occupancy/retention), mirror those values in evidence_report.evidence.md_metrics, and separate MD-supported observations from the literature-calibrated affinity direction.

references.note: Morton, Baase & Matthews 1995 - alkylbenzene binding to the T4L L99A cavity.
Merski et al. 2015 - matched L99A apolar-ligand complexes.
